source_excerpt: We conclude that, in the field of public education, the doctrine of 'separate but equal' has no place. Separate educational facilities are inherently unequal.
notes: The source explicitly limits its holding to 'the field of public education,' whereas the claim asserts the ruling applies 'across all public facilities generally.'
